name: From Montanejos to Montán – Gravel circular by the Arenoso Reservoir
name_original: De Montanejos a Montán – Gravel circular por el Embalse de Arenoso
region_intro: Touring cycling around Montanejos offers routes through a landscape characterized by rugged mountains, deep gorges carved by the Mijares River, and extensive pine forests. The region features winding mountain roads and rocky terrain, providing diverse environments for cycling. Notable natural features include the Arenoso Reservoir and the thermal springs of Fuente de los Baños. This area is ideal for cyclists seeking varied elevations and scenic natural backdrops. Best touring cycling routes around Montanejos The most popular touring cycling route is From Montanejos to Montán – Gravel circular by the Arenoso Reservoir, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route offers gravel sections and views of the Arenoso Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Montanejos Hot Springs – Arenos Reservoir loop from Montanejos, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This route includes the thermal springs area and varied terrain around the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Montanejos – Caudiel Washhouse loop from Montanejos, a 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail leading through the Alto Mijares region, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Montanejos is defined by rugged mountains, the Mijares River gorges, and pine forests. The network offers options for both challenging climbs and scenic rides through natural landscapes. The routes in Montanejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 240 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Montanejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos offers a diverse network of over 25 touring cycling routes. These routes traverse rugged mountains, deep gorges carved by the Mijares River, and extensive pine forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Montanejos?
A: The months of May and June are particularly popular for touring cycling in Montanejos. The region's climate during this period is generally favorable, allowing cyclists to fully enjoy the diverse landscapes and natural features.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in Montanejos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Montanejos are predominantly challenging, with 22 out of 25 routes rated as difficult. There are also 3 routes of moderate difficulty, but currently, there are no easy routes specifically for touring cyclists listed in this guide. Expect significant elevation gains and varied terrain.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Montanejos?
A: Yes, Montanejos features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934265972/from-montanejos-to-montan-gravel-circular-by-the-arenoso-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Montanejos to Montán – Gravel circular by the Arenoso Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanejos Hot Springs – Arenos Reservoir loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the thermal springs area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanejos – Caudiel Washhouse loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the Alto Mijares region.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along the touring cycling routes?
A: Montanejos is rich in natural beauty. You can visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt;, known for its natural thermal springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt; offers dramatic gorges, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful waterfall where you can enjoy a refreshing swim. Many routes also offer views of the Arenoso Reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on touring cycling routes in Montanejos?
A: Cyclists in Montanejos can expect a mix of rugged mountains, winding mountain roads, and sections through pine forests. The region is characterized by its dramatic gorges carved by the Mijares River and rocky terrain. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934265972/from-montanejos-to-montan-gravel-circular-by-the-arenoso-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Montanejos to Montán – Gravel circular by the Arenoso Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, also feature gravel sections.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or water activities near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt; offers natural thermal springs with a constant warm temperature, perfect for a refreshing stop. The Arenoso Reservoir is also known for water sports like kayaking and paddle surfing, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; provides a natural pool for swimming.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Montanejos?
A: The touring cycling routes around Montanejos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 240 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs and scenic natural backdrops.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest to explore by bike?
A: Yes, parts of the renowned Camino del Cid, a cultural tourist itinerary, pass through Montanejos, offering gravel and MTB options. You can also explore settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2779054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; itself, which are points of interest in the region.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes in Montanejos?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, touring cycling routes in Montanejos often feature significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20139554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenos Reservoir – Montanejos Hot Springs loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1270 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanejos – Caudiel Washhouse loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 1145 meters.

Q: Are there any specific services for cyclists in Montanejos?
A: Yes, Montanejos is cyclist-friendly. Establishments like Campuebla Apartamentos & Spa provide valuable services such as bicycle rental, washing areas, self-workshops, guarded parking, and showers. The BTT Montanejos Association also actively promotes mountain biking and offers information on local routes and events.

Q: Can I find mountain biking options in Montanejos?
A: While this guide focuses on touring cycling, Montanejos is also a prime location for mountain biking. The Alto Mijares MTB Center alone offers 8 signposted routes covering over 266 km, suitable for both cycle touring and more demanding mountain biking on forest tracks and natural paths.
tips:
Emblematic area of the town of Montanejos. It is a thermal water spring at a constant temperature of 25ºC, which will allow you to take a pleasant bath at any time of the year.
The site also has a rest area, with picnic areas and children's games.

This reservoir is located on the course of the Mijares river, one of the most picturesque in the province of Castellón given its surroundings and landscape. Downstream is the town of Montanejos with many activities and services.
